title: SSRF bypass via redirect: card fetcher follows redirects to internal hosts
description: CARD_CLIENT uses redirect::Policy::limited(5) but validate_outbound_url() is only called on the initial URL before the request is made. If an attacker controls a page at https://evil.com that 302-redirects to http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ (or any internal IP), reqwest will follow the redirect without re-validating the target. The federation client correctly uses Policy::none() to avoid this. Fix: either disable redirects (Policy::none()) and handle manually with re-validation at each hop, or use reqwest's redirect::Policy::custom() with a closure that calls validate_outbound_url on each redirect target.
